sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After DOWN x2:
- Player at rows 36-39, cols 16-23 (white at 16-20, green at 21-23)
- Center blue square frame at rows 36-39, cols 32-35 (node 7)
- Center blue center at rows 37-38, cols 33-34 (node 9)
- Left blue center at rows 37-38, cols 17-18 (node 8) - inside white extension
- Left blue square frame at rows 28-31, cols 24-27 (node 1)
- Bar at rows 28-31, cols 28-39 (node 2)
- Carried blue center at rows 29-30, cols 25-26 (node 3) - inside left blue square frame
So the player moved DOWN to rows 36-39. The white extension is still on the LEFT (cols 16-20), and the left blue center moved with the player to rows 37-38, cols 17-18.
Now the player is at the same row level as the center blue square (rows 36-39). The center blue square frame is at cols 32-35. The player is at cols 16-23. I need to move RIGHT to reach the center blue square.
But the player is facing LEFT (white extension on the left). To collect the center blue center, I need the player to be to the LEFT of the frame, facing RIGHT. So I need to flip the player's direction.
Actually, wait. The player is at cols 16-23, and the center blue square frame is at cols 32-35. The player is to the LEFT of the frame. If I move RIGHT, the player would approach the frame from the left. But the white extension is on the LEFT side, so the player is facing LEFT (away from the frame).
Hmm, but earlier when the player was at the right blue square, the white extension was on the RIGHT, and the player was to the LEFT of the frame. SPACE extended the white part to the right, collecting the blue center. So the player was facing RIGHT (toward the frame).
In this case, the player is to the LEFT of the center blue square frame, but facing LEFT (away from the frame). So I need to flip the player's direction to face RIGHT.
But how do I flip the direction? Earlier, the player flipped from facing RIGHT to facing LEFT when it moved LEFT past the left blue square frame. So maybe I need to move RIGHT past the center blue square frame to flip the direction.
But the frame is at cols 32-35, and the player is at cols 16-23. If I move RIGHT, the player would approach the frame. When the player overlaps with the frame, maybe the direction flips.
Let me try moving RIGHT to approach the center blue square frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at rows 36-39, cols 16-23 (white at 16-20, green at 21-23)
# Center blue square frame at rows 36-39, cols 32-35
# Player facing LEFT (white on left), need to face RIGHT to collect
# Let me move RIGHT toward the center blue square

result = action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After RIGHT x3:")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] in ['N', 'W', 'b', 'c', 'g']:
        print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, boundary={node['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
